title: Geniostoma venosum
intro: Geniostoma venosum, also known as the 'Fiji Jasmine', is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Loranthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Geniostoma venosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
